title: Isla Makes New Friends
synopsis: When Isla moves to a new neighborhood, she feels shy about making friends at the playground. With encouragement from her mom and her own brave heart, Isla discovers that friendship can bloom when you're kind and open to others. A heartwarming story about overcoming shyness and finding belonging through shared play and kindness.

pages.text: Isla looked out her new bedroom window. Everything was different here. The trees were taller. The houses were a different color. Even the birds sounded different. 'I miss my old friends,' Isla whispered to her stuffed bunny.

illustration.description: Isla with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes looking sadly out a bedroom window, holding a stuffed bunny, with moving boxes around the room and a sunny playground visible outside
illustration.alt: Isla looking out window at new playground

pages.text: Mom knocked on the door. 'Want to explore the playground across the street?' she asked. 'I see lots of kids playing there.' Isla's tummy felt nervous butterflies, but she nodded. Maybe it would be fun.

illustration.description: Isla with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes sitting on her bed while her mom stands in the doorway smiling, with a bright sunny playground visible through the window showing diverse kids playing
illustration.alt: Mom talking to Isla about the playground

pages.text: At the playground, Isla sat on a bench with Mom. She watched kids laughing on the swings. They were sliding down the big yellow slide. Some were playing tag near the monkey bars. 'They all look like they're having so much fun,' Isla said.

illustration.description: Isla with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes sitting on a bench with her mom, watching diverse kids playing on bright colorful playground equipment with swings, slides, and monkey bars in primary colors
illustration.alt: Isla and mom watching kids play at playground

pages.text: 'Why don't you go say hello?' Mom suggested gently. Isla shook her head. 'What if they don't want to play with me? What if they think I'm weird?' The butterflies in her tummy fluttered even more.

illustration.description: Close-up of Isla with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes looking worried while her mom puts a comforting hand on her shoulder, with joyful energetic playground activities happening in the bright sunny background
illustration.alt: Isla looking nervous about joining the other kids

pages.text: Just then, a small boy with curly black hair tripped and scraped his knee near the slide. He started to cry. Other kids gathered around, but nobody knew what to do. Isla's heart told her to help.

illustration.description: A small boy with curly black hair sitting on the ground holding his scraped knee while diverse kids stand around looking concerned, with Isla with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes watching from nearby on the bright sunny playground
illustration.alt: Boy with scraped knee surrounded by concerned children

pages.text: Isla walked over slowly. 'Are you okay?' she asked the boy. 'I have a band-aid in my mom's purse.' The boy looked up with teary eyes. 'That would help a lot,' he sniffled. 'I'm Marcus.'

illustration.description: Isla with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes kneeling down next to Marcus who is holding his knee, while other diverse kids watch as she offers help on the inclusive playground with primary colored equipment
illustration.alt: Isla helping Marcus with his scraped knee

pages.text: Mom brought over the band-aid, and Isla carefully put it on Marcus's knee. 'There! All better,' she said with a smile. A girl with braided hair came over. 'That was really nice of you! I'm Sofia. Want to play with us?'

illustration.description: Isla with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes putting a band-aid on Marcus's knee while Sofia with braided hair and other diverse kids watch approvingly on the bright sunny playground with joyful energetic atmosphere
illustration.alt: Isla putting band-aid on Marcus while Sofia introduces herself

pages.text: 'Really?' Isla's eyes lit up. 'I'm Isla. I just moved here and don't know anyone yet.' 'Well, now you know us!' Marcus said, standing up and testing his knee. 'We were about to play freeze tag. Do you want to join?'

illustration.description: Isla with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes looking excited while surrounded by diverse kids including Marcus and Sofia on the playground filled with primary colors, swings, slides, and white space creating a welcoming scene
illustration.alt: New friends inviting Isla to play freeze tag

pages.text: Soon Isla was running and laughing with her new friends. They played freeze tag, took turns on the swings, and went down the slide together. The nervous butterflies in her tummy had turned into happy bubbles.

illustration.description: Isla with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes laughing and running with diverse kids playing freeze tag on the bright sunny playground with colorful swings and slides, showing friendship activities and joyful energy
illustration.alt: Isla playing freeze tag with her new friends

pages.text: When it was time to go home, Sofia ran up to Isla. 'Will you come back tomorrow?' she asked. 'Yes!' Isla grinned. 'I can't wait!' As she walked home with Mom, Isla felt warm and happy. Making new friends wasn't so scary after all.

illustration.description: Isla with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes waving goodbye to her diverse group of new friends at the playground while walking away with her mom, with the inclusive playground showing laughter and friendship in bright primary colors and white space
illustration.alt: Isla saying goodbye to her new friends

metadata.illustrationStyle: bright sunny playground, diverse kids playing, primary colors, white space, joyful energetic, inclusive, park swings slides, laughter, friendship activities

questions.question: How did Isla feel when she first saw the new playground?
questions.explanation: Isla felt nervous butterflies in her tummy when she saw all the kids playing and wasn't sure if they would want to be her friend.

questions.question: What happened to Marcus at the playground?
questions.explanation: Marcus tripped and scraped his knee near the slide, which is when Isla decided to help him.

questions.question: How did Isla help Marcus?
questions.explanation: Isla offered to get a band-aid from her mom's purse and carefully put it on Marcus's scraped knee.

questions.question: Who invited Isla to play freeze tag?
questions.explanation: Sofia said 'Want to play with us?' and then Marcus mentioned they were about to play freeze tag and asked if Isla wanted to join.

questions.question: How did Isla feel at the end of the story?
questions.explanation: Isla felt warm and happy as she walked home with Mom, realizing that making new friends wasn't so scary after all.
